titles.title: cowfootR: An R Package for Dairy Farm Carbon Footprint Assessment

citation.article_title: How does co-product handling affect the carbon footprint of milk? Case study of milk production in new zealand and sweden
citation.author: Flysjö
citation.journal_title: International Journal of Life Cycle Assessment
citation.unstructured_citation: Flysjö, A., Cederberg, C., Henriksson, M., & Ledgard, S. (2011). How does co-product handling affect the carbon footprint of milk? Case study of milk production in new zealand and sweden. International Journal of Life Cycle Assessment, 16, 420–430. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11367-011-0283-9

citation.article_title: A common carbon footprint approach for the dairy sector: The IDF guide to standard lifecycle assessment methodology
citation.author: International Dairy Federation
citation.unstructured_citation: International Dairy Federation. (2022). A common carbon footprint approach for the dairy sector: The IDF guide to standard lifecycle assessment methodology. International Dairy Federation.

citation.article_title: 2019 refinement to the 2006 IPCC guidelines for national greenhouse gas inventories
citation.author: IPCC
citation.unstructured_citation: IPCC. (2019). 2019 refinement to the 2006 IPCC guidelines for national greenhouse gas inventories (E. Calvo Buendia, K. Tanabe, A. Kranjc, J. Baasansuren, M. Fukuda, S. Ngarize, A. Osako, Y. Pyrozhenko, P. Shermanau, & S. Federici, Eds.). Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change; IPCC.

citation.article_title: Cradle-to-farmgate analysis of milk carbon footprint: A descriptive review
citation.author: Pirlo
citation.unstructured_citation: Pirlo, G. (2012). Cradle-to-farmgate analysis of milk carbon footprint: A descriptive review. Italian Journal of Animal Science, 11, 109–118. https://doi.org/10.4081/ijas.2012.e20

citation.article_title: The carbon footprint of milk production on a farm
citation.author: Stolarski
citation.unstructured_citation: Stolarski, M. J., Warmiński, K., Krzyżaniak, M., Olba-Zięty, E., & Dudziec, P. (2025). The carbon footprint of milk production on a farm. Applied Sciences, 15, 8446. https://doi.org/10.3390/app15158446

citation.article_title: Reducing food’s environmental impacts through producers and consumers
citation.author: Poore
citation.unstructured_citation: Poore, J., & Nemecek, T. (2018). Reducing food’s environmental impacts through producers and consumers. Science, 360(6392), 987–992. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aaq0216
